W.reason: No quality-loop artifacts provided beyond code and README.

X.reason: Artifacts are present and contain some specific details (e.g., broken-pipe panic fix, manual smoke commands), but rely heavily on standard shell output piping descriptions rather than deep analysis.

Y.reason: No quality-loop artifacts provided beyond code and README.

Z.reason: Artifacts are highly detailed, including specific commands, expected outputs, build logs, and precise notes on build cycle warnings/fixes and an architectural limitation (%CPU in single-shot).
